entradas.titulo: Gustavo Leal F. *: Primer año de la política de salud de la administración de Sheinbaum
entradas.resumen: Su nota básica es  la continuidad sobre el legado de la administración AMLO (2018-2024) operada vía los Cien Pasos para la Transformación y la República Sana que, ciertamente, no constituyen una propuesta nueva en materia sanitaria: trabajan sobre lo heredado, sobre un lienzo ya tendido. Tampoco las nuevas iniciativas presidenciales portan cambio alguno ni alteran esa estructura heredada: como Salud Casa por Casa o La Clínica es Nuestra.

entradas.titulo: CROC respalda relección de Isaías González; busca emular a Fidel Velázquez
entradas.resumen: Dirigentes de la Confederación Revolucionaria de Obreros y Campesinos (CROC) alentaron, desde ahora, la continuidad de Isaías González Cuevas como secretario general para el periodo 2028-2034.

entradas.titulo: Sellan acuerdo Morena, PT y PVEM para dar salida a plan B
entradas.resumen: Tras largas horas de negociación, que se prolongaron desde la mañana del viernes hasta las primeras horas de este sábado, los partidos de la coalición Morena, PT y PVEM avanzaron anoche en la Secretaría de Gobernación en los detalles para cerrar un acuerdo de unidad que dé luz verde al plan B de la presidenta Claudia Sheinbaum en materia electoral.

entradas.titulo: Hugo Aboites*: Situam, un renovado actor universitario
entradas.resumen: Las y los académicos  de la UAM tienen un contrato colectivo sumamente generoso para el quehacer docente y de investigación. Libertad de investigación, sabático, licencias, equilibrio entre docencia e investigación, etcétera. Además, la administración de la UAM se ha comprometido con el Situam por contrato colectivo de trabajo a informar sobre las actividades académicas de la institución. De esa manera el sindicato puede intervenir con mayor información para la defensa de los trabajadores académicos y para que éstos puedan aportar sobre la orientación y marcha de este importante quehacer.

entradas.titulo: Inmegen: cientos de variantes genéticas influyen en la obesidad en México
entradas.resumen: Cientos de variantes genéticas influyen en la susceptibilidad a desarrollar obesidad en la población mexicana, aunque por sí solas no determinan que una persona padezca esta enfermedad, aseguró Lorena Orozco, investigadora emérita y líder del Laboratorio de Inmunogenómica y Enfermedades Metabólicas del Instituto Nacional de Medicina Genómica (Inmegen).

entradas.titulo: Chen Daojiang*: Autonomía y apertura para construir juntos el futuro - Con motivo de la celebración de las Dos Sesiones de China
entradas.resumen: Durante las reuniones  anuales de la Asamblea Popular Nacional (APN) y la Conferencia Consultiva Política del Pueblo Chino (CCPPCh), que también se llaman Dos Sesiones, el canciller Wang Yi respondió preguntas de medios chinos y extranjeros sobre la política exterior y las relaciones internacionales de China, exponiendo de manera sistemática la posición de China en temas relevantes como la cooperación en Asia-Pacífico, las relaciones China-ALC y la construcción de una comunidad de futuro compartido para la humanidad. Sus palabras no sólo reflejaron la dirección general de la diplomacia china, sino que también trazaron caminos concretos de cooperación entre China y todo el mundo.

entradas.titulo: Gloria Muñoz Ramírez: Los de abajo
entradas.resumen: “Para mí es una alegría ir a la milpa a cosechar lo que voy a comer y lo que va a comer mi familia. Me gusta escarbar la tierra con mis manos para llevar alimento a mi mesa. Eso es lo que defendemos”, dice Neify, del colectivo Múuch Xíinbal, de Quintana Roo.

entradas.titulo: Taxis de aplicación tendrán espacio de corta estadía en AICM: titular de Marina
entradas.resumen: Manzanillo, Col., El secretario de Marina, Raymundo Pedro Morales, aseguró ayer que los taxis de aplicación tendrán un espacio de corta estadía en el Aeropuerto Internacional de la Ciudad de México (AICM), en un terreno que ocupaba la Secretaría de la Defensa Nacional.

entradas.titulo: Simplifican trámites de refugio en la Comar
entradas.resumen: El próximo viernes entran en vigor cambios administrativos que buscan simplificar los trámites más recurrentes a cargo de la Comisión Mexicana de Ayuda a Refugiados (Comar). Según el acuerdo publicado en el Diario Oficial de la Federación, se eliminan requisitos y se establecen acciones de simplificación y mejora de procedimientos que se realizan ante ese organismo dependiente de la Secretaría de Gobernación.En el documento firmado por la responsable de la política interna del país, Rosa Icela Rodríguez, se incluyen cambios a 17 asuntos, entre ellos ocho en mejoras de trámites y en un número igual se generan formatos a fin de agilizar los procesos. Los trámites previos a este acuerdo se resolverán conforme a las leyes y reglamentos vigentes al momento de su presentación.

entradas.titulo: Salida amistosa al caso Bonfilio Rubio Villegas, plantea Encinas a familiares
entradas.resumen: Durante la audiencia que se celebró ayer ante la Comisión Interamericana de Derechos Humanos (CIDH) sobre el caso de Bonfilio Rubio Villegas, el indígena nahua que fue víctima de ejecución extrajudicial por parte del Ejército en junio de 2009, en la cual el Estado mexicano reconoció parcialmente su responsabilidad en los hechos y se dijo dispuesto a llegar a un acuerdo amistoso con la familia del agraviado.

entradas.titulo: Modelo de la 4T reduce pobreza y violencia: Presidenta
entradas.resumen: Manzanillo, Col., La presidenta Claudia Sheinbaum Pardo afirmó ayer que el modelo económico impulsado por los gobiernos de la Cuarta Transformación ha demostrado que no sólo reduce la pobreza, sino también la violencia en el país.

entradas.titulo: SRE destaca cooperación y confianza
entradas.resumen: La administración Trump sabe que hay cooperación en materia de combate al narcotráfico y crimen organizado con Estados Unidos, pero “siempre con pleno respeto a nuestra soberanía e integridad territorial, con confianza mutua y sin subordinar”, señaló la Secretaría de Relaciones Exteriores (SRE).

entradas.titulo: Revelan lista de jubilados de empresas estatales
entradas.resumen: La Secretaría Anticorrupción y Buen Gobierno puso a disposición de la ciudadanía el listado completo de personas jubiladas de empresas estatales y banca de desarrollo, junto con las cantidades que reciben, no sólo de los ex funcionarios y altos mandos con pensiones millonarias.

entradas.titulo: Sentencia EU a 15 años de cárcel a mexicano por tráfico de armas
entradas.resumen: Un ciudadano mexicano fue sentenciado ayer a 180 meses de prisión por conspiración para traficar armas de fuego desde Estados Unidos hacia México, informó el Departamento de Justicia estadunidense.

entradas.titulo: Raúl Romero*: Crimen organizado y capitalismo
entradas.resumen: Es un error común y  un error inducido pensar que narcotráfico y crimen organizado son lo mismo; y que por su carácter “ilegal”, tanto narcotráfico como crimen organizado son anomalías que el sistema combate para garantizar legalidad y seguridad. El narcotráfico es entendido como el tráfico de drogas ilegales, que implica cultivo, fabricación, distribución y venta. El crimen organizado, por su parte, se refiere a “una organización criminal continua que opera racionalmente para lucrar” con actividades como tráfico de drogas, trata de mujeres, tráfico de migrantes, venta de órganos, venta ilegal de armas de fuego, tráfico ilegal de recursos naturales y fauna silvestre, lavado de dinero, falsificación de productos, extorsión y cobros por protección, por mencionar algunos.

entradas.titulo: Solicitan grupos civiles a la CIDH vigilar el desplazamiento forzado interno en el país
entradas.resumen: Organizaciones civiles y víctimas solicitaron a la Comisión Interamericana de Derechos Humanos (CIDH) que vigile la situación de desplazamiento forzado interno en México, debido a que esta problemática persiste “y se ha expandido” en el país, ya que entre 2016 y 2024 –afirmaron– se documentó que 390 mil personas se vieron obligadas a abandonar sus hogares.

entradas.titulo: “Vamos a ver cómo responden”, dice Sheinbaum
entradas.resumen: Manzanillo, Col., “Vamos a ver”, respondió la presidenta Claudia Sheinbaum Pardo cuando se le preguntó si el plan B tendrá el mismo destino que la reforma electoral –rechazo–, por tratarse de modificaciones constitucionales.

entradas.titulo: Sarampión sigue al alza; en una semana, 865 casos más, reporta la Ssa
entradas.resumen: La cifra de casos confirmados de sarampión en el país sigue en aumento, con 6 mil 792 casos de enero a la fecha, al corte del jueves; es decir 865 contagios más respecto al 5 de marzo, con un total de 13 mil 244 casos desde 2025. Los decesos se mantienen en 34, de los cuales siete corresponden a este año.

entradas.titulo: Critica Trump rechazo de México a su “ayuda” contra el narcotráfico
entradas.resumen: El mandatario de Estados Unidos, Donald Trump, declaró que la presidenta de México, Claudia Sheinbaum, “no debió haber rechazado mi ayuda” sobre el combate a los cárteles del narcotráfico en territorio mexicano.

entradas.titulo: Sheinbaum: no está bien informado; sí existe colaboración
entradas.resumen: Tecomán, Col., La presidenta Claudia Sheinbaum Pardo afirmó ayer que, en la cooperación con Estados Unidos en materia de seguridad, el respeto a la soberanía nacional no está sujeto a negociación.

entradas.titulo: Gustavo Gordillo / II: ¿Y ahora qué sigue?
entradas.resumen: Irán. Todos sabían, en   medio de la incertidumbre de la guerra contra Irán, cuatro cosas. Nunca se ha tumbado a un régimen político desde el aire con bombas sino que se requiere insurrección interna y fuerzas externas invasoras en el terreno. Segundo, la guerra contra Irán desequilibraría dramáticamente el mercado energético. Tercero, lejos de terminar con la inestabilidad política,social y militar en el Medio Oriente, se incrementaría. Y cuarto, los ganadores de una guerra de Estados Unidos e Israel contra Irán serían los regímenes autócratas de Irán, Israel, China y, sobre todo, Rusia. Los perdedores, los pueblos de Irán, Israel, Ucrania y la democracia estadunidense. Más los líderes políticos europeos, cada vez más chiquitos e insignificantes. Salvo Pedro Sánchez, de España. Todo esto está pasando en términos reales.

entradas.titulo: Exhibe guerra contra Irán grietas en la economía de Estados Unidos
entradas.resumen: Washington. La resiliente economía de Estados Unidos ya mostraba señales de tensión incluso antes del inicio de la guerra contra Irán, según datos publicados ayer, lo que subraya los riesgos que puede plantear el aumento de los precios de la gasolina y la energía.

entradas.titulo: AL posee minerales estratégicos para transiciones digital y energética: BID
entradas.resumen: América Latina cuenta con un acervo estratégico en minerales críticos para la transición energética y la transformación digital. Los países de la región cuentan con reservas de cobre, litio, grafito, níquel y tierras raras, cuya importancia es equiparable a la del petróleo en el siglo pasado, destacó el Banco Interamericano de Desarrollo (BID) en un informe.

entradas.titulo: Activa SHCP subsidio al diésel
entradas.resumen: Ante la escalada de los precios del petróleo, que esta semana han superado los 100 dólares por barril, la Secretaría de Hacienda y Crédito Público activó ayer el subsidio al impuesto especial sobre producción y servicios (IEPS) que se aplica al diésel, y dejó fuera las gasolinas.

entradas.titulo: Carlos Fernández-Vega: México SA
entradas.resumen: ¿Qué fue de la sucia campaña,   rebosante de clichés de la guerra fría y financiada por la mano que mece a la oposición, la cual histéricamente pregona: “¡cuidado!”, porque los médicos cubanos que arriban a México sólo lo hacen “para inocular el comunismo” y “financiar al régimen totalitario de la isla”, al tiempo que “cuestiona” si en realidad se trata de galenos o “agentes de la seguridad de Cuba”. Y como éstas, muchísimas más sandeces inundan los medios de “comunicación” al servicio de Washington y la ultraderecha. Entonces, ¿dónde acabó esta tentativa? Pues en el mismo sitio de donde salió: la basura, como tantas otras “cruzadas” emprendidas por los neofascistas.

entradas.titulo: Combatirán la violencia digital hacia las mujeres
entradas.resumen: El gobierno federal firmó un acuerdo con las plataformas digitales Google, Meta y TikTok para combatir la violencia digital contra las mujeres, mediante el reforzamiento de políticas que alerten de riesgos en estos espacios y alentando una atención más ágil a las denuncias hechas en estas plataformas y su vinculación a otros procesos legales. Durante […]

entradas.titulo: Por unanimidad, avalan en el Senado la iniciativa contra pensiones doradas
entradas.resumen: El Senado de la República aprobó por unanimidad la reforma al artículo 127 constitucional, que acaba con las pensiones millonarias que perciben ex funcionarios de empresas paraestatales, banca de desarrollo y otros organismos públicos, al establecer un límite a esos ingresos “estratosféricos”. Con 116 votos de senadores de todas las fuerzas políticas se avaló esa […]

entradas.titulo: Habrá «costo político» para Verde y PT por no apoyar la reforma electoral: Morena
entradas.resumen: Luego de que ayer fue rechazada en la Cámara de Diputados la iniciativa de reforma electoral de la presidenta Claudia Sheinbaum, el grupo parlamentario de Morena definió dos pasos inmediatos: reconstruir su relación con los partidos del Trabajo (PT) y Verde Ecologista de México (PVEM), los cuales –a su juicio– “sí tendrán un costo político” […]

entradas.titulo: Key UAE Port Suspends Oil Loadings After Drone Attack, Fire
entradas.resumen: The United Arab Emirates suspended loading operations at the key oil-trading hub of Fujairah after a drone strike and fire on Saturday, demonstrating the vulnerability of the country’s only export route while the Strait of Hormuz is blocked.

entradas.titulo: Trump’s War Jolts Global Central Banks From Fed to ECB to BOJ
entradas.resumen: Central banks from Washington to London to Jakarta are about to make their first assessments of economic damage after more than two weeks of conflict between the US and Iran.

entradas.titulo: JPMorgan Makes Bold Push to Offload Huge LBO Debt
entradas.resumen: Jamie Dimon has been warning for weeks — months, even — that the credit cycle will eventually sour. Now, his bank is about to test investors’ appetites.

entradas.titulo: Bloomberg This Weekend 3/14/2026
entradas.resumen: The news doesn’t stop when markets close. Hosts David Gura, Christina Ruffini and Lisa Mateo bring clarity, context and a bit of humor to the weekend’s biggest headlines, LIVE from New York. Joining the show today, AP International Correspondent Philip Crowther, Former Member of National Security Council Col. Joel Rayburn (Ret.), Axios Energy Reporter Ben Geman, International Maritime Organization Secretary General Arsenio Dominguez, Head of Petroleum Analysis at GasBuddy Patrick De Haan, Former Department of Energy Secretary Ernest Moniz, and Trane CEO Dave Regnery.  (Source: Bloomberg)

entradas.titulo: Gas Prices & Airfare Spike as Iran War Drags On
entradas.resumen: Patrick De Haan, GasBuddy's Head of Petroleum Analysis, on "Bloomberg This Weekend" predicts gas prices will continue rising as demand increases this spring. (Source: Bloomberg)

entradas.titulo: US Faces Tough Choices in Iran War's Next Steps
entradas.resumen: "You set things in motion but you can't control how they end up," Sir Lawrence Freedman, emeritus professor of war studies at King's College London, warns of the consequences of the US military strikes and the perilous paths ahead - on "Bloomberg This Weekend." (Source: Bloomberg)

entradas.titulo: Iran Oil Hub Strike Raises Fears of Further Supply Upheaval
entradas.resumen: A major US attack on the island that exports the bulk of Iran’s crude is raising fears of more widespread supply disruptions in the region, further straining oil and gas markets already in upheaval after two weeks of war in the Middle East.

entradas.titulo: Pointed News Quiz | Oil, Asia Politics, Hollywood
entradas.resumen: David Gura, Christina Ruffini, and Lisa Mateo of “Bloomberg This Weekend” play Pointed! Wager your points, leverage your bets and answer wisely. A new quiz is available to play each week on Bloomberg.com (Source: Bloomberg)

entradas.titulo: Trane CEO on "Bloomberg This Weekend" C-Suite Saturdays
entradas.resumen: Dave Regnery, CEO of Trane Technologies, discusses Trane's commitment to sustainability, and growing from a consumer heating and cooling company to leader in accommodating the massive surge in data centers. (Source: Bloomberg)

entradas.titulo: 'Iran Crossed A Red Line': Former Energy Secretary on Iran's Nuclear Program
entradas.resumen: Former US Secretary of Energy Ernesto Moniz tells "Bloomberg This Weekend" that Iran has already accumulated enough nuclear material to build weapons - but it's unclear where it is and what to do with it.  (Source: Bloomberg)

entradas.titulo: Volatile & Dangerous Game': Trump Balancing Iran Military Campaign Without Disrupting Oil Supply
entradas.resumen: On "Bloomberg This Weekend," Axios Energy Reporter Ben Geman predicts more uncertainty in the energy market after the US strike on Iran's Kharg Island. (Source: Bloomberg)

entradas.titulo: Top UN Official on Risk to Oil Tankers and Ships in Strait of Hormuz
entradas.resumen: Secretary-General Arsenio Dominguez, of the UN's International Maritime Organization, tells "Bloomberg This Weekend" that more than a dozen ships have been damaged during the US-Iran War, with many more trapped without fresh food and water.  (Source: Bloomberg)

entradas.titulo: Tensions Rising on Strait of Hormuz After US Attacks on Strategic Island
entradas.resumen: Live reports from "Bloomberg This Weekend" from the United Arab Emirates and Israel as ship traffic stalls at the Strait of Hormuz. Overnight the Trump Administration bombed the strategic Kharg Island, which handles roughly 90% of Iran's oil production. (Source: Bloomberg)

entradas.titulo: Greek Oil Tanker En Route to Russia Attacked in Black Sea
entradas.resumen: A Greek oil tanker was damaged by a projectile or drone in the Black Sea as it sailed toward Russia, Greece’s shipping ministry said.

entradas.titulo: Scottie Pippen Helps Sell Wall Street on Prediction Markets
entradas.resumen: At a finance conference in Boca Raton, Florida, when Wall Street traders weren't shooting hoops with Scottie Pippen, they were talking about prediction markets and what they might mean for the industry (Source: Bloomberg)

entradas.titulo: Malaysia Graft Chief Alleged to Hold Millions in Shares, MP Says
entradas.resumen: A Malaysian lawmaker said a probe into the shareholdings of the country’s anti-graft chief Azam Baki allegedly uncovered that he owned shares worth 14 million ringgit ($3.6 million) in nine companies.

entradas.titulo: Why Sweden Is Becoming a Defense Powerhouse as Europe Rearms
entradas.resumen:
Sweden is known for its social welfare system and has a long history of neutrality, but as Europe races to rebuild its military strength after Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, the Nordic country is emerging as an important player. We take a look at how Saab and Sweden’s broader defense ecosystem have become central to Europe’s rearmament, why the country was better prepared than many of its neighbors, and what its European peers can learn from it. 
 (Source: Bloomberg)

entradas.titulo: Second Dynacom Oil Tanker Passes Hormuz as Other Owners Hold Back
entradas.resumen: A Greek shipowner sent a second oil tanker through the Strait of Hormuz, bucking caution among the shipping industry as Iran lashes out across the region in response to attacks by Israel and the US.

entradas.titulo: Thames Water Creditors Pitch Improved Rescue Offer to Ofwat
entradas.resumen: Thames Water’s creditors have pitched an improved rescue offer for the troubled utility that includes a bigger equity injection and an increase in committed debt, according to a person with knowledge of the matter.

entradas.titulo: Coming up on The Weekend Show | Strikes on Iran Continue
entradas.resumen: The news doesn’t stop when markets close. Hosts David Gura, Christina Ruffini and Lisa Mateo bring clarity and context to the weekend’s biggest headlines, LIVE from New York. (Source: Bloomberg)

entradas.titulo: Trump Orders Strikes on Kharg Island, a Vital Hub for Iran’s Oil
entradas.resumen: However, Trump said that “for reasons of decency,” he chose “NOT to wipe out the Oil Infrastructure on the Island.”

entradas.titulo: Six U.S. Troops Killed in Aircraft Crash in Iraq
entradas.resumen: Trump maintains that the United States is winning its war against Iran.

entradas.titulo: The Economic Costs of the Iran War, by the Numbers
entradas.resumen: From multimillion-dollar munitions to surging oil prices, here’s how much the war is costing.

entradas.titulo: Iran War Puts India in Tricky Position
entradas.resumen: Modi’s government faces increasing domestic dissent when it comes to working with the Trump administration.

entradas.titulo: What in the World?
entradas.resumen: Test yourself on the week of March 7: The war in Iran continues, conflict flares in South Sudan, and José Antonio Kast is inaugurated.

entradas.titulo: In Putin’s Russia, Resistance Is Lonely
entradas.resumen: An Oscar-nominated documentary offers an intimate view of Russia’s descent into fascism.

entradas.titulo: Christian Democracy Is Facing Regime Change
entradas.resumen: Europe’s unique political tradition is under threat—and perfectly suited for the post-liberal moment.

entradas.titulo: Two Books About the Pull of Home
entradas.resumen: Plus, more international fiction releases in March.

entradas.titulo: 5 Streaming Movies to Help Understand Iran Now
entradas.resumen: Many of these filmmakers have found themselves scrutinized by government censors, leading to arrests and imprisonment.

entradas.titulo: How the Climate Won Paris’s Culture War
entradas.resumen: Plenty of issues are on the ballot in this month’s mayoral election. The city’s green transition isn’t one of them.

entradas.titulo: Can Sierra Leone Still Keep Its Mothers Alive?
entradas.resumen: What foreign aid cuts mean in practice in one African country.

entradas.titulo: What China’s Five-Year Plan Says About Its Economy
entradas.resumen: A growth target of 4.5 percent is the lowest in decades.

entradas.titulo: Trump’s Summit Offers More Deference Than Details
entradas.resumen: Leaders emerged from the “Shield of the Americas” with few policy specifics.

entradas.titulo: The Iran War Could Spark a New Era of Global Shiite Extremism
entradas.resumen: Degrading command and control over proxy groups could make them more dangerous and unpredictable.

entradas.titulo: Why Europe’s Approach to Defending Democracy Is Failing
entradas.resumen: Bromides about the “center holding” miss the point.

entradas.titulo: Matthew Kroenig and Trita Parsi Debate the War in Iran
entradas.resumen: Will the campaign advance U.S. interests in the region or set them back?

entradas.titulo: Iran’s Strait of Hormuz Toolkit: Drones, Missiles, and Mines
entradas.resumen: A breakdown of the tools Iran has to maintain pressure on Trump.

entradas.titulo: Iran’s New Supreme Leader Vows to Continue Blocking the Strait of Hormuz
entradas.resumen: Tehran escalates Gulf strikes and warns of future attacks on U.S. bases in the region.

entradas.titulo: Trump’s Last-Gasp Foreign Policy
entradas.resumen: Ill-considered overreach in Iran and elsewhere signals imminent decline.

entradas.titulo: Trump’s Efforts to Defuse the Oil Spike Aren’t Working
entradas.resumen: Big reserve releases, promises of escorts, and insurance can’t convince the market that the crisis will be over soon.

entradas.titulo: Real Madrid beat Elche to pressure Barcelona for La Liga title
entradas.resumen: Federico Valverde scores his fifth goal in three games as Real Madrid beat Elche 4-1 to move one point behind Barcelona.

entradas.titulo: US-Israeli strike kills 15 at Isfahan factory, Iranian media says
entradas.resumen: Iranian media report the deaths in central Iran as Tehran launches new missile salvoes at Israeli targets.

entradas.titulo: Thousands in Madrid protest ‘forgotten’ Gaza, warn Iran war may spiral into
entradas.resumen: Thousands rallied in central Madrid calling for an end to the war involving the United States, Israel and Iran.

entradas.titulo: Hundreds protest against US-Israeli strikes on Iran in Seoul
entradas.resumen: Hundreds of peace activists and demonstrators marched in central Seoul on Saturday in protest of the US-Israeli strikes.

entradas.titulo: Three members of Iranian women’s football team pull Australia asylum bids
entradas.resumen: Seven members of the Iranian football delegation sought asylum, with four now known to have withdrawn applications.

entradas.titulo: Arsenal beat Everton as Dowman makes Premier League history
entradas.resumen: Arsenal beat Everton 2-0 in a nervy match in the Premier League as they continue their pursuit of the title.

entradas.titulo: Trump administration to drop charges against US veteran who burned flag
entradas.resumen: Veteran Jay Carey burned a US flag in protest of an executive order from Trump calling for prison terms for such acts.

entradas.titulo: Turkey on alert as Iran conflict escalates, warns against provocations
entradas.resumen: Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan says Ankara is monitoring the regional war involving Iran, the US, and Israel.

entradas.titulo: UNESCO condemns strike on Minab girls’ school: ‘They attacked twice… How
entradas.resumen: Secretary of Iran’s National Commission for UNESCO, condemned a missile strike on a girls’ primary school in Minab.

entradas.titulo: Six killed in attacks on Ukraine as EU extends sanctions against Russians
entradas.resumen: EU maintains pressure after slamming US for lifting sanctions on Russian oil exports as Middle East war bites.

entradas.titulo: Trump administration threatens news outlets over critical coverage of Iran
entradas.resumen: FCC chair says news outlets that share 'distortions' about US war with Iran could lose their licences.

entradas.titulo: Iranian woman explains ‘Allahu Akbar’ to Americans, amid explosion in Iran
entradas.resumen: Video shows an Iranian woman recording a message to Americans, amid an explosion in Iran.

entradas.titulo: Israeli settlers shoot Palestinians in West Bank villages, steal livestock
entradas.resumen: One Palestinian reported killed in Qusra, as others injured in Rashayda, amid surge in Israeli settler violence.

entradas.titulo: US embassy in Venezuela reopens as Trump pushes for access to resources
entradas.resumen: Trump administration has pressed the interim Venezuelan government for concessions on issues such as oil access.

entradas.titulo: Iran warns UAE ports and ‘American hideouts’ are targets
entradas.resumen: An Iranian military spokesperson warned that ports, docks and “American hideouts” in the UAE could be targets.

entradas.titulo: Is the world ignoring Gaza?
entradas.resumen: Amid the US and Israel's war on Iran, we discuss the impact on diplomatic efforts and support for Palestine.

entradas.titulo: Protesters in Cuba attack Communist party office in rare riot over blackout
entradas.resumen: Anti-government protesters in Cuba sparked an outbreak of violence amid worsening blackouts and food shortages.

entradas.titulo: Trump says ‘many countries’ will send warships to Hormuz amid Iran blockade
entradas.resumen: US president urges naval coalition as Iran says Strait of Hormuz closed to 'tankers and ships of enemies' during war.

entradas.titulo: UN chief Guterres says ‘no military solution, only diplomacy’ for Lebanon
entradas.resumen: Sources report Lebanon preparing delegation for talks with Israel, while Guterres drums up humanitarian support.

entradas.titulo: Protests erupt in Cuba as US restrictions spark food, energy shortages
entradas.resumen: Authorities say a local communist party office was lit on fire during rare antigovernment demonstration on the island.

entradas.titulo: How the war on Iran struck the shores of Sri Lanka
entradas.resumen:
There are places in the world where you expect war to intrude. And then there are places where its arrival feels surreal.
For me, the Sri Lankan city of Galle has always belonged firmly to the latter category. The old fort, perched above the Indian Ocean, has the feel of a tropical cousin to Cartagena’s walled city. Inside its ramparts is an improbable mix: Western expatriates, boutique hotels, a long-standing local Muslim community and wealthy Sri Lankans who have restored colonial houses into...

entradas.titulo: American flag raised at US embassy in Venezuela for first time since 2019
entradas.resumen:
The American flag was raised on Saturday over the US embassy in Venezuela for the first time since 2019, a move that highlighted the recent shift in relations between the two countries since then President Nicolas Maduro was captured by American troops in January.
Though the flag is now waving, the building is undergoing renovations and it remains unclear when it will fully reopen.
The move comes after several statements from US President Donald Trump in support of Maduro’s successor, acting...

entradas.titulo: Jurgen Habermas, the philosopher who shaped Germany’s post-war conscience, dies at 96
entradas.resumen:
Jurgen Habermas, best known for his theory of political consensus-building, shaped the discourse of post-war ⁠Germany more than any other popular intellectual.
He died on Saturday, aged 96, in Starnberg, ⁠Germany, the publisher Suhrkamp said.
Over the course of seven decades, his public interventions – from searing critiques of fascist thought in the 1950s to more recent warnings against resurgent militarism and nationalism in Germany – steered the country at critical junctions.
Not only his...

entradas.titulo: Israel may commit ‘new genocide’ in Lebanon using Iran war as pretext, Turkey warns
entradas.resumen:
Turkey expressed deep concern on Saturday over Israel’s ongoing strikes on Lebanon, voicing fears it could commit “a new genocide” under the guise of fighting Iran-backed Hezbollah.
“We are frankly concerned [Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin] Netanyahu is moving toward a new genocide under the pretext of fighting Hezbollah,” Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan told a news conference.
“The international community must act as soon as possible in the face of the crimes that Israel continues to...

entradas.titulo: Iran war: Trump urges China, other nations, to send warships to secure Hormuz
entradas.resumen:
US President Donald Trump on Saturday urged other nations, including China, to deploy ships to help secure the Strait of Hormuz, a key route for global oil supplies disrupted by the war in the Middle East.
“Many Countries, especially those who are affected by Iran’s attempted closure of the Hormuz Strait, will be sending War Ships, in conjunction with the United States of America, to keep the Strait open and safe,” Trump wrote in a Truth Social post.
“Hopefully China, France, Japan, South Korea,...

entradas.titulo: Iran war entering ‘decisive phase’, Israel says, as attacks continue
entradas.resumen:
Israel said the war against Iran was entering its “decisive phase” on Saturday, as explosions rocked cities across the Middle East, with strikes on the US embassy in Baghdad and a major Emirati energy facility.
Washington’s embassy in Iraq was hit by a drone, security sources said, the second time it had been targeted since February 28, when the United States and Israel attacked Iran and plunged the Gulf into a conflict that has sent shock waves through the global economy.
Millions have been...

entradas.titulo: UN chief says ‘diplomatic avenues are available’ to end war in Lebanon
entradas.resumen:
UN chief Antonio Guterres has said that diplomatic channels remain open to end the war between Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah and urged the international community to support Lebanon.
“There is no military solution, only diplomacy, dialogue and full implementation of the UN Charter and Security Council resolutions. The diplomatic avenues are available, including through my special coordinator for Lebanon ... and through key member states,” he said on Saturday during a visit to Beirut.
Lebanon...

entradas.titulo: Protesters in Cuba attack Communist party office amid blackout fury
entradas.resumen:
Anti-government protesters attacked a ⁠Communist party office in northern Cuba early on Saturday, a ⁠state-run newspaper reported, in a rare outburst of public dissent triggered by worsening blackouts that have been exacerbated by a US oil blockade.
A rally against power cuts and food shortages appeared to begin peacefully in the city of Moron late on Friday then turned violent in the early hours of Saturday morning, Invasor newspaper said.
Videos on social media showed a large fire and ‌people...

entradas.titulo: UK eyes ‘targeted’ support for households amid rising energy costs
entradas.resumen:
The British government is looking to provide “targeted” support for ⁠poorer households to offset the impact of ⁠surging energy costs due to the conflict ⁠in the Middle East, finance minister Rachel Reeves has said.
In an interview published on Saturday, Reeves told the Times newspaper the government was looking at options to help those vulnerable to sharp rises in energy prices, especially those who relied on heating oil, but ruled out universal help for all households, saying it would be...

entradas.titulo: Hamas calls on Iran to ‘avoid targeting neighbouring countries’
entradas.resumen:
Palestinian Islamist movement Hamas on Saturday called on Iran to refrain from targeting neighbouring countries, while affirming Tehran’s right to defend itself against Israel and the United States.
In a rare appeal, Hamas also urged the international community to take steps to end the war that has gripped the Middle East since it began on February 28.
“While affirming the right of the Islamic Republic of Iran to respond to this aggression by all available means in accordance with international...

entradas.titulo: Indian sailors stranded by Iran war just want to go home: ‘there is a lot of anxiety’
entradas.resumen:
Stranded for two weeks at the Iranian port of Bandar Abbas as drones and missiles set ships ablaze nearby, 26-year-old seafarer Ambuj says he has not been home for six months and cannot wait to see his family.
Ambuj, who asked Reuters not to use his second name for security reasons, is one of about 23,000 Indians working on merchant, harbour and offshore vessels across ‌the wider Gulf region, which is bearing the brunt of the war between Iran and the United States and Israel.
Hundreds of tankers...

entradas.titulo: Aid groups warn of humanitarian crisis in Lebanon as nearly 1 million displaced by war
entradas.resumen:
Fatima Nazha slept on the streets for two days after she and her family fled their home in Beirut’s southern suburbs following an Israeli mass evacuation order.
All of the schools the government turned into shelters were full, and the family could not afford a hotel or a flat, so she and her husband eventually moved into a tent in the country’s biggest stadium while their children and grandchildren found shelter near the southern coastal city of Sidon.
In just 10 days, more than 800,000 people...

entradas.titulo: By waging war on Iran, Trump leaves the US economy more vulnerable
entradas.resumen:
War in the Middle East is again producing an oil shock, as was the case in past years. For the chief perpetrator of the new shock – the United States – this will be a multi-front war where the financial impact could hit the country harder than import price shocks.
The US is a debtor nation on a grand scale, running as it does both current account and budget deficits and therefore being highly dependent upon foreign capital inflows. Will the Trump administration’s antics – widely perceived as...

entradas.titulo: Iran war fallout puts 9 million Indian workers in the line of fire
entradas.resumen:
When the alert arrives on his phone, Yashwant Deshmukh knows exactly what to do.
The Dubai-based political analyst moves away from the windows, waits for the second message confirming the missile has been intercepted, and then goes back to work.
“It has become a drill,” he said.
For the roughly 9 million Indians living and working across the Gulf, the war on Iran has shattered one of the region’s most durable illusions: that cities like Dubai, Abu Dhabi, Doha and Manama would remain apart from...

entradas.titulo: Palestinian actor Motaz Malhees blocked from Oscars by Trump travel ban
entradas.resumen:
Palestinian actor Motaz Malhees said a travel ban imposed by Donald Trump’s administration is preventing him from attending Sunday’s Academy Awards, whose nominees include a movie in which he has a starring role.
The Voice ‌of Hind Rajab, a film about a five-year-old Palestinian girl killed by Israeli forces in Gaza in 2024, has been Oscar-nominated for best international feature film.
Malhees, who plays the role of a call centre operator attempting to help her, said he cannot attend the Academy...

entradas.titulo: US moves 2,000 Marines from Japan to Middle East as Iran war intensifies
entradas.resumen:
The US military has started moving at least one amphibious assault ship and more than 2,000 Marines from Japan to the Middle East as the US-Israeli war against Iran continues, according to US media reports.
With the war about to enter its third week and US President Donald Trump vowing to ramp up military action against Iran, The Wall Street Journal and Fox News said on Friday the military was sending the Okinawa-based 31st Marine Expeditionary Unit to the region.
The assault ship Tripoli,...

entradas.titulo: Top Chinese entrepreneurs commit to Middle East expansion despite Iran war
entradas.resumen:
Two prominent Chinese business leaders have pledged to double down on outbound investment – including in the Middle East and Latin America – even as geopolitical risks intensify following the outbreak of the US-Israeli war with Iran and Washington’s increasing interventions in the western hemisphere.
Li Dongsheng, founder and chairman of Chinese electronics giant TCL, said the recent crisis in the Middle East had only a temporary impact on the company’s operations there and that the overall...

entradas.titulo: Strikes on Iraqi capital kill 2 Iran-linked Kataeb Hezbollah members
entradas.resumen:
Explosions rocked Iraq’s capital Baghdad on Saturday after two strikes targeted the powerful Iran-backed group Kataeb Hezbollah, killing two members including a “key figure”, security sources said.
The strikes are the first in the heart of Baghdad to kill Iran-backed fighters since the start of the Middle East war on February 28, which has not spared Iraq, long a proxy battleground between the US and Iran.
“At 2.15am, a missile struck a house that was being used by Kataeb Hezbollah,” said a...

entradas.titulo: Up to 16,000 Meta jobs at stake as Zuckerberg focuses on AI
entradas.resumen:
Meta is planning sweeping lay-offs that could affect 20 per cent or more of the company, three sources familiar with the matter said, as it seeks to offset costly artificial intelligence infrastructure bets and prepare for greater efficiency brought about by AI-assisted workers.
No date has been set for the cuts and the magnitude has not been finalised, the people said.
Top executives have recently signalled the plans to other senior leaders at Meta and told them to begin planning how to pare...

entradas.titulo: Trump tells South Korea he is only leader who can break North Korean deadlock
entradas.resumen:
South Korean Prime Minister Kim Min-seok and US President Donald Trump discussed the possible reopening of talks between Trump and North Korean leader Kim Jong-un in a meeting in Washington, Yonhap news agency reported on Saturday.
Kim told Trump he was the only Western leader to have had dialogue with North Korea’s Kim and was currently the only person who could resolve issues on the Korean peninsula, Yonhap quoted Kim as telling reporters in Washington.
“President Trump said he was curious if...

entradas.titulo: Trump asegura que muchos migrantes en EE.UU. son "malos" por culpa de sus genes
entradas.resumen: "Hay algo que no está bien ahí. Su genética no es exactamente la misma que la tuya", declaró el mandatario estadounidense.

entradas.titulo: VIDEO: Ministra mexicana dobla su ropa en plena sesión de la Suprema Corte
entradas.resumen: En una sesión de la Suprema Corte de Justicia de la Nación de México, la ministra Lenia Batres fue blanco de críticas, después de que fuera grabada cuando doblaba una prenda de vestir mientras otros ministros intervenían en la discusión pública del órgano judicial. Los usuarios de las redes sociales cuestionaron el nivel de seriedad de Batres, al tratarse de un debate en el máximo tribunal del país.

entradas.titulo: Reportan que Irán suma 336 horas sin internet
CGRI: "Aún no hemos bloqueado militarmente el estrecho de Ormuz, solo lo controlamos"
entradas.resumen: El comandante de la Armada del Cuerpo de la Guardia Revolucionaria Islámica se refirió al reclamo del presidente de EE.UU. a otros países para que ayuden a garantizar el tráfico en esa ruta fluvial.

entradas.titulo: "Para la violencia no habrá impunidad": Díaz-Canel comenta los hechos vandálicos en la ciudad de Morón
entradas.resumen: Un grupo de manifestantes lanzó piedras contra la sede del comité municipal del Partido Comunista cubano.

entradas.titulo: VIDEOS: Arde una base de EE.UU. en Irak atacada por grupos proiraníes
entradas.resumen: Se reporta una nueva ola de ataques de grupos locales proiraníes contra la base estadounidense Victoria en Irak, situada cerca del aeropuerto de Bagdad. Medios difunden videos no verificados en los que se aprecia fuego y humo causados por los impactos.

entradas.titulo: Masacre en un balneario de Ecuador: sicarios asesinan a seis jóvenes en una fiesta
entradas.resumen: El ataque armado ocurrió en una vivienda alquilada para celebrar una graduación en el balneario de Playas, en la provincia de Guayas. Entre las víctimas hay dos menores de edad.

entradas.titulo: MINUTO A MINUTO: Irán amplía sus represalias ante los llamados al mundo  de Trump para proteger Ormuz
entradas.resumen: El presidente estadounidense afirmó que varios países afectados por la crisis que generó el bloqueo del estrecho de Ormuz enviarían sus buques de guerra para desbloquearlo.

entradas.titulo: General iraní: "El fin de la guerra está en nuestras manos"
entradas.resumen: Omán e Irán son, junto a otros países del golfo Pérsico, los que deben controlar el estrecho de Ormuz, opina un infuyente general de la Guardia Revolucionaria Islámica.

entradas.titulo: Irán golpea una base que alberga cazas F-35 de EE.UU.
entradas.resumen: El CGRI detalló que la operación se realizó mediante un ataque combinado de misiles de combustible líquido y solido.

entradas.titulo: Israel running critically low on interceptor missiles — portal
entradas.resumen: According to the news portal, Israel has entered the current military operation already low on interceptors, because they were actively used during last summer’s conflict with Iran

entradas.titulo: One more drone downed en route to Moscow
entradas.resumen: Saturday’s drone attack on the Moscow Region surrounding the Russian capital has become the largest since the start of the year

entradas.titulo: Two houses damaged by Ukrainian shelling attack on DPR in past day
entradas.resumen: No casualties were reported

entradas.titulo: Drone attack on Moscow becomes largest this year — TASS calculations
entradas.resumen: On-duty air defenses destroyed 65 drones in less than 12 hours

entradas.titulo: Iran’s IRGC tells residents living near US industrial sites in Middle East to leave
entradas.resumen: Iran’s elite army unit also warned all US businesses across the region to evacuate

entradas.titulo: Russian skier Voronchikhina picks up her second gold medal at Italy’s Paralympics
entradas.resumen: The Russian Paralympic athlete won the women's slalom standing ahead of China’s Wenjing Zhu who took silver

entradas.titulo: Iran dismisses Ukraine’s drone support to US as 'a joke' — diplomat
entradas.resumen: Earlier, Vladimir Zelensky announced sending drone teams to the Middle East, namely Qatar, the UAE, and Saudi Arabia

entradas.titulo: Air defenses down another 15 drones targeting Moscow — mayor
entradas.resumen: First responders are working at the scene where the debris fell

entradas.titulo: Russia downs 280 Ukrainian drones during 10 hours on Saturday
entradas.resumen: Over the Bryansk Region alone, air defenses, Russia’s National Guard and the BARS-Bryansk brigade destroyed as many as 128 enemy fixed-wing UAVs, according to Governor Alexander Bogomaz

entradas.titulo: Tehran, Washington were able to make good progress prior to attacks — Iran’s top diplomat
entradas.resumen: Washington seems to have misinterpreted the stance of the Islamic Republic, Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said

entradas.titulo: US rebuffs efforts by Middle East monarchies toward ceasefire talks with Iran — Reuters
entradas.resumen: In its turn, Iran is not ready to discuss a ceasefire until the United States and Israel stop intense bombing and meet its demands, including compensation

entradas.titulo: Israel carries out 400 waves of attacks on Iran since February 28 — IDF
entradas.resumen: The Israeli Air Force focused "on the dismantling of infrastructure and targeting operatives of the fire, defense, and production units in western Iran," the Israeli military said

entradas.titulo: EU moves to prolong its Russia blacklist until mid-September
entradas.resumen: According to the document, "In the context of the sanctions' review, the Council also decided not to renew the listings of two individuals and to remove five deceased persons from the list"

entradas.titulo: Lebanese death toll from Israeli attacks rises to more than 800 — ministry
entradas.resumen: The number of those injured is 2,000, the Arab republic’s Ministry of Public Health said

entradas.titulo: Iran announces new wave of missile strikes on Israel — public broadcaster
entradas.resumen: Air raid sirens have been ringing out in Tel Aviv and adjacent areas in central Israel

entradas.titulo: Iran attacks US military objects in UAE, Kuwait, Bahrain — IRGC
entradas.resumen: Iranian missiles and UAVs struck Patriot radars, a control tower, and anti-aircraft installations at the Al-Dhafra base, the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps reported

entradas.titulo: Middle East conflict may lead to 70% drop in oil production, expert says
entradas.resumen: Even if Russia provides additional supplies, importers will be able to offset only part of the potential losses, Aditya Saraswat noted

entradas.titulo: Pentagon reports strikes on 90 military targets on Iran's Kharg Island
entradas.resumen: But the Islamic Republic’s oil industry facilities located there were not affected, CENTCOM said

entradas.titulo: Israeli army reports destruction of Iranian Space Agency research center
entradas.resumen: In addition, strikes targeted several facilities involved in the production of air defense systems, the military stated

entradas.titulo: Ukraine loses around 1,135 troops in special op zone in one day
entradas.resumen: Russian air defenses shot down 10 guided bombs and 285 fixed-wing drones of the Ukrainian army over the past day

entradas.titulo: Trump: Iran war will end when I ‘feel it in my bones’
entradas.resumen: The president dismissed reports that the U.S. was facing a munitions shortage.

entradas.titulo: Richard Grenell to leave role as Kennedy Center head
entradas.resumen: Trump says Matt Floca, the venue’s vice president of facilities, will take over the role.

entradas.titulo: The great Russian disconnect
entradas.resumen: Silicon Valley is pulling the plug. The Kremlin is locking the doors. Inside the race to cut Russia off from the global internet — and what comes after.

entradas.titulo: EU agrees on rollover of Russia sanctions
entradas.resumen: Two names were removed from the list to secure an agreement on Saturday.

entradas.titulo: Anti-doping agency to consider barring Trump, US officials from Olympics
entradas.resumen: The World Anti-Doping Agency is weighing a rule change because the U.S. has refused to pay its dues to the agency since 2023, the Associated Press reported.

entradas.titulo: Ghislaine Maxwell still seeking Trump pardon, her lawyer says
entradas.resumen: Maxwell’s lawyer spoke at a white-collar crime conference with Nicolás Maduro’s attorney.

entradas.titulo: US court blocks probe of Fed chair Jerome Powell
entradas.resumen: The decision potentially clears the path for Trump's nominee to replace Powell as Fed chair, former Fed Gov. Kevin Warsh.

entradas.titulo: Carney joins European leaders in criticizing US easing of Russian oil sanctions
entradas.resumen: “Canada's position is to maintain sanctions on Russia,” Prime Minister Mark Carney said today in Norway.

entradas.titulo: France offers to broker Israel-Lebanon talks
entradas.resumen: Lebanon is willing to engage in "direct talks" with Israel, the French president said.

entradas.titulo: US embassy in Baghdad hit by missile
entradas.resumen: A helipad inside the U.S. embassy compound was struck early Saturday, the AP reported.
